# orcasecurity_integration_azure_sentinel (Resource)

Manages a [Microsoft Sentinel](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/)
(Azure Sentinel) integration in Orca Security.

Under the hood this creates an entry under `POST /api/external_service/config`
with `service_name = "azure_sentinel"`. The Log Analytics workspace primary key
is stored in Orca's secret store and is never returned by the API.

## Example Usage

### Basic example

```terraform
# Azure Sentinel integration
resource "orcasecurity_integration_azure_sentinel" "example" {
  template_name = "Azure Sentinel name"
  log_type      = "OrcaAlerts"
  workspace_id  = "workspace_id"
  primary_key   = var.azure_sentinel_primary_key
  is_enabled    = true
  is_default    = false

  business_units = [
    "d7a3b159-3063-433b-b954-0586ff3e8438",
    "5308642b-f207-4610-a13b-f39c4db4a7a3",
  ]
}
```

### Sourcing the primary key from HashiCorp Vault

```terraform
data "vault_generic_secret" "sentinel" {
  path = "secret/azure-sentinel/orca"
}

resource "orcasecurity_integration_azure_sentinel" "from_vault" {
  template_name = "sentinel-prod"
  log_type      = "OrcaAlerts"
  workspace_id  = data.vault_generic_secret.sentinel.data["workspace_id"]
  primary_key   = data.vault_generic_secret.sentinel.data["primary_key"]
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `template_name` — (Required, String) Identifier for the integration as shown
  in the Orca UI. Changing this forces a new resource.
* `log_type` — (Required, String) Custom log type name written to the Log
  Analytics workspace (for example, `OrcaAlerts`).
* `workspace_id` — (Required, String) Azure Log Analytics workspace ID backing
  the Sentinel instance.
* `primary_key` — (Required, String, Sensitive) Azure Log Analytics workspace
  primary key. Stored in Orca's secret store; never returned by the API.
* `is_enabled` — (Optional, Bool) Defaults to `true`.
* `is_default` — (Optional, Bool) Defaults to `false`.
* `business_units` — (Optional, Set of String) Optional set of Orca business
  unit IDs that may use this integration.

## Attribute Reference

* `id` — (String) Orca external service config identifier (UUID).

## Notes

* Orca strips `primary_key` from `GET` responses (stored in SSM). The provider
  keeps the value already in Terraform state to avoid permanent diffs. Rotate
  through `terraform apply`; for out-of-band rotation use
  `terraform apply -replace=orcasecurity_integration_azure_sentinel.example`.
* Use an encrypted remote backend (S3 + KMS, GCS, Terraform Cloud, etc.) and
  restrict read access to the state.

## Import

```bash
terraform import orcasecurity_integration_azure_sentinel.example "Azure Sentinel name"
```

After import, set the `primary_key` argument in your configuration — the API
does not return it.
